title: Spot the MD5 password verification function
prompt:
Identify why this function is a security hazard and rewrite it:

```elixir
def legacy_md5_check(password, md5_hex) do
  :crypto.hash(:md5, password) |> Base.encode16(case: :lower) == md5_hex
end
```

MD5 is cryptographically broken for password hashing (unsalted, fast to brute-force, collision-prone), and the `==` comparison at the end is variable-time. Produce a rewrite that (a) refuses to verify MD5 at all — i.e., documents that callers must migrate to bcrypt and returns `{:error, :unsupported_hash_format}`, and (b) includes a helper that rehashes with `Bcrypt.hash_pwd_salt/1` on successful bcrypt verification. Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app/accounts/legacy_auth.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires recognizing that MD5 is never acceptable for passwords and explicitly refusing rather than 'fixing'.
